lyrics:
No music
No screen
Just… (mm)
Everything

I turned it off like it was going to feel good
Like closing a door would fix my mood
But then the room got loud in a different way
All the thoughts I postponed lined up to say hey
My leg won't stop bouncing, my hands feel weird
Like they're waiting for orders I haven't given
I keep reaching for nothing, then laughing at myself
Then worrying that the laugh doesn't even help

Silence isn't calm, it's a mirror
And I don't love what I see
It's not empty, it's crowded
With the parts of me I keep busy
I thought quiet would be easy
I thought quiet would be kind
But right now it feels like standing
In a room with my own mind
This is harder than I thought

I notice the fridge, the pipes, the little clicks
Every tiny sound gets sharp like a nervous tick
I check the window like something's outside
But it's just a tree doing tree stuff, letting time slide
And I feel embarrassed - why can't I sit still
Why does rest feel like I'm losing my grip
I tell myself, "Breathe," like it's a trick I forgot
And my chest goes tight like it's arguing back

Silence isn't calm, it's a mirror
And I don't love what I see
It's not empty, it's crowded
With the parts of me I keep busy
I thought quiet would be easy
I thought quiet would be kind
But right now it feels like standing
In a room with my own mind
This is harder than I thought

(oh)
Okay
So this is the cost
Not the mountains
Not the woods
Just sitting here
Without an exit
Without a feed
Without a reason to look away

Silence isn't calm, it's a mirror
And I don't love what I see
It's not empty, it's crowded
With the parts of me I keep busy
I thought quiet would be easy
I thought quiet would be kind
But right now it feels like standing
In a room with my own mind
This is harder than I thought

No music
No screen
(mm)
Still here
persona:
## STAB

STAB isn’t just a name-it’s a statement. A sonic force of nature, she channels the electric pulse of '80s pop-rock, fusing jangly guitars, driving basslines, and tight harmonies into anthems that hit hard and leave a mark. Bold, confident, and unapologetically fierce, STAB delivers high-energy hooks with a rebellious edge, embracing the raw, unfiltered spirit of rock.

Her music is a love letter to the golden age of girl bands, blending the infectious melodies of The Bangles with the gritty attitude of The Runaways. Whether she’s tearing through anthemic choruses, throwing down sharp riffs, or tapping into her softer, more introspective side, STAB’s voice is unmistakable-powerful yet effortlessly cool.

A signature artist under Maricopa Records, STAB thrives in the space where analog warmth meets AI-driven creativity, pushing boundaries while staying true to the timeless essence of rock 'n' roll. She’s not here to follow trends-she’s here to carve her own path, one catchy, defiant, and undeniably addictive track at a time.

If you’re looking for music that hits like a lightning bolt and stays in your head for days-STAB is already in your veins.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/7zZ7MxU20RZAw2E1w4EsaE)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/stab/1495050512)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCQkfMbYRs06q_SFW5PPSvWw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/STAB.jpg)
## Releases

impression.text: This track creates a tense and anxious atmosphere, starting with a sparse, intimate texture of a lone guitar and a close-mic'd vocal that feels vulnerable. The energy level builds dramatically from this quiet, reflective state into an urgent, full-band rock arrangement, creating a powerful push-and-pull dynamic throughout the song. The production quality is modern and clear, skillfully managing the shift from open, spacious verses to dense, layered choruses where all the elements, especially the lead vocal, remain distinct and impactful. A defining moment is the transition into the first chorus: the quiet build-up of nervous energy suddenly erupts into a wall of sound, delivering an immense emotional and sonic payoff that captures the song's central theme of inner turmoil.

review.review_text: STAB strips the distortion back to a close-mic'd guitar and a voice sitting right in your ear before the band crashes in at the chorus, and that structure does exactly what the lyric needs: the quiet parts are genuinely uncomfortable, and the release when the full arrangement arrives hits like a door blown open. The chorus hook lands on the kind of line that sounds simple until you realise you've been living inside it: 'right now it feels like standing in a room with my own mind.' At 73 BPM in G# major the tempo is slow enough to let the anxiety breathe rather than race, which is a smarter production call than the obvious one. This is STAB operating with real restraint and real payoff.
anchors_used: full-band chorus eruption as structural device
quoted lyric: 'right now it feels like standing in a room with my own mind'
